medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Over the last couple of years, her vision had ‘not been great’. Formal visual ﬁeld testing revealed a heteronymous inferior quadrantanopsia suggestive of damage to the superior chiasmatic ﬁbers. She did not experience headache and an endocrinological evaluation was normal.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Magnetic resonance imaging of the brain revealed a suprasellar mass lesion with a solid, enhancing component surrounded by large cysts ﬁlled with CSF-isointense material. A biopsy was performed. Hematoxylin and Eosin stained sections disclosed vaguely lobular and trabecular arrangements of epithelioid cells in a background of hyalinized vessels, frequently cuﬀed by mature lymphocytes and plasma cells. The tumor cells contained one or a few ovoid nuclei with open ﬁnely dispersed chromatin and pinpoint nucleoli. The nuclei were surrounded by ﬁne and pale eosinophilic cytoplasm, which featured subplasmamembrane oedema, imparting focally prominent ‘lacunar’ appearance. No foci of necrosis were seen and no mitoses were identified. The tumor was strongly positive for glial ﬁbrillary acidic protein (GFAP), establishing a diagnosis of chordoid glioma of the third ventricle.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Vaguely lobular and trabecular arrangements of epithelioid cells in a background of hyalinized vessels, frequently cuﬀed by mature lymphocytes and plasma cells. The tumor cells contained one or a few ovoid nuclei with open ﬁnely dispersed chromatin and pinpoint nucleoli. The nuclei were surrounded by ﬁne and pale eosinophilic cytoplasm, which featured subplasmamembrane oedema, imparting focally prominent ‘lacunar’ appearance. No foci of necrosis were seen and no mitoses were identified.
The tumor was strongly positive for GFAP.

radiographic_examinations.procedure_name: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the brain
radiographic_examinations.findings: Suprasellar mass lesion with a solid, enhancing component surrounded by large cysts filled with CSF-isointense material.

other_tests.findings: Heteronymous inferior quadrantanopsia suggestive of damage to the superior chiasmatic fibers.
other_tests.changes_over_time: Serial visual field examinations without deterioration.
